We're excited.

So you can understand from the get-go what you'll be, you know, encountering with us.
So that's okay.
I think notetakers, yeah, both are in.

So please let me know once you are looking at my screen.
Yep, I can see everything fantastic.
Love it.
Cool.
So this is a small agenda.
Basically, we're going to meet the team right now.
The next immediate thing we'll be doing, we're going to look into the project overview, the Facebook breakdown, and then we're going to dive specifically into your project.

It looks like there's some internet stability on my end.
One second.
I thought it was me.
Yeah, me too.
But we all lose Augusto.
I got a little notification saying that it was probably me, but I hear everything perfectly fine, so feel free to continue.
Okay, let's keep him.
him.
A minutes.
just texted him to see if he can rejoin the call.
Oh, perfect.
I did not see that Augusto is still frozen.
Renato, I understand that you're from Chile, right?
Well, I'm half Chilean.
My father's from Chile, yeah.
Oh, great.
mother's Dutch, my dad's Chilean, yeah.
Well, Camila and Juan Pablo are both from Chile.
I'm in the first place.

Igualmente.

was, or it's like, I'm 30 minutes north from Amsterdam.
Yeah, so prior to moving here, we moved out of here.

We moved here during the pandemic and I've been here since.
It's different from the United States.
It's a lot safer with kids, especially these days.
Yeah, it's very different places, but both of them are very cool, I think.

Santiago.
Santiago?
On a way.

Mostly, mostly over there in Viña, they live over there as well.
Nice to see.
Nice.

Yes, like temporarily, like for two months.

Just working from Mexico City?
Yes.
I would say that those are the small treats that you have when you work remotely.
So I came here to Mexico City for two months because I fell in love with the food, so I needed to try it a little bit more.
I fully understand.
I'm going back to Santiago next month.
You've got to stay for longer, man.
Just stay the rest of the year.
That's true.
you much.
Thank

You are very right, yes.
It's crazy the amount of restaurants and food at the street stalls that you can find all over the place.
It's like full of them.
Yeah, I've seen like 100 tacos in a week in your social media.
My guys are the best.
Sorry, team, but I'm not sure what's happening with Augusto, so I'm going to just take over until he joins back.
As we were doing the review of the call, we're going to also take a look at the phase breakdown of the project so we know what's going to happen at what point.
And then we're going to dive into a couple of questions and review the branding process itself, and at the end, go through the next steps.
So, let me see.
So, I know we've met already a little bit.
I'm going to introduce
Myself, I'm Jannia.
I'm the head of project management here.
I'm going to be working with Augusto, who's going to be your main point of contact.
We're basically going to follow up with you in terms of where we are at, what needs to happen next, etc.
So really excited to start working today.
From the admin team, I think you've met Lea.
She's our GM, but we also have Hazel when it comes to finance and Michaela when it comes to sales.
In your particular case, I know this is kind of an exchange for services, so this wouldn't apply, but just in any case, those are your two points of contact in terms of admin.
Then you have Juan, who you met already, and Kami, who you've met already as well.
I don't know if you have anything else to mention at this point.
No?
Good.
Amazing.
All right, so the general overview of the project.
I understand right now we're going to be working only on branding.
This process should take about one month and a half to become...
Tweet it.
Augusto, you're back.
Or maybe not.
Wi-Fi being a little odd today.
Okay.
Would you like me to continue?
Would you like to take over with the rest of the presentation?
Oh, I'll just keep going.
It would be better if you...
Right.
So, as I was saying, the entire brand new process is going to take about one point and a half months.
So, it is going to be done in the following way.
We're going to be doing the kickoff session, which is what we're going through today.
Today, we're going to be looking at the questionnaire you answer.
And we're also going to be asking you questions to go deeper into what we need to do moving forward.
Then, have the first phase, which is...
Good boarding.
This should take about a week.
If we have more rounds of feedback, it could take a bit longer, but it should be a process that guides us on to the next phase, which is concepting.
This phase should take about two weeks, ultimately, and here we're going to land a little bit more on your brand and how it is going to look in general.

After that, can go on to phase three, where we're going to dive deeper into the brand identity itself, and once this is approved and landed where we want, we go on to the brand guidelines, which takes about two weeks to be completed.
At the end, you get the figure file, a PDF, and anything you need in terms of assets.
Any questions here or anything the team would like to add?
No questions on my end.
Amazing.
Okie dokie.
So, I understand with GrowthX from MBD, we've been working on blog posts, we've been doing a bit of...
Positioning of the side, I understand the company in general, and I think the team does as well.

We want to ask questions in terms of what you're looking to solve with this rebrand, what are the main pain points that you're facing at this moment with your brand, and in general, if you can go through these questions and help us out, that would be great.
Yeah, absolutely.
What problem does our company solve?

Well, currently, you know, they're still figuring that out, and in all honesty, it's like, there's a multitude of problems, but I think what our company is, is similar to you, we deliver services, first and foremost, so growth marketing, content marketing services at scale, and we do that at AI first.
We have a bunch of pods of marketing professionals that are using proprietary, mostly workflows to do growth marketing.
Performance marketing, SEO marketing, extremely fast and at a higher quality.
So that's what we're doing right now.
That's what we're doing for customers and what the majority of customers will know us for.
At the same time, we are building the software to do all of this at scale, right?
So the infrastructure and the tools that these marketing professionals will use in order to do their work that much faster is AI-enabled workflow software.
And our philosophy there is that, you know, like we really require people to be in the loop there.
I was like, technology is not at a point where we can create content that is at a quality where we can just autonomously let AI do whatever it wants to do.
There is a significant amount of quality control.
Quality prompting required to get to certain outcomes, and we built the software to do that.
So that software is still in progress.
We just started out really laying the foundational infrastructure for that, but the idea is that that software part will become larger eventually.
Now, whether that software will be launched under the same moniker or the same brand as growthx is still something that we're discussing internally.
We think it may be something different.

However, I do want to operate from a core set of brand pillars that will influence everything that we will do on the line, whether it's growthx or spin-off brands, there needs to be, you know, it's like a certain, a certain core common design DNA that I want to operate from that we can.

We used to at least make better informed decisions down the line, and for me, this is started out by, you know, it's like sending over some of the pillar work that we've done so far, it's mostly creating alignment with folks around, hey, you it's like, what do we like, what should this represent, what are the words that are connected to that overall, and done some of that.
So, let me just grab one of the documents that I had over here, send it over.
So, I took the majority of the team through, yeah, a simple brand pillar exercise that I do with, like, I've done it.
Thank you.
Thank
countless times with founders, and that's what I sent over to you, so it's the growthx brand, the creative breed, essentially.
And the most important thing to get from there is just the overall presentation values that we ended up on.
So there is, and you see that in the current growthx brand as well, there's a certain weight and a certain gravitas to the logo already that we want to maintain.
And that's, we captured that through bold, it needs to be bold, you know, it's like we're a marketing company, we need to capture attention.
So it can be a confident logo overall.
You got there, which is prominent, vivid, glowing, luminescent, it needs to be, it needs to be there.
Then we landed on the experimental, we came there through avant-garde, creative, unexpected, unique, different, fresh.
Nonconforming, mysterious, contrasting, revolutionary.

We are somewhat of an odd duck and like an underdog, both in terms of how we operate as well as our perception within Silicon Valley, for example, because the majority of companies are software-only companies and we do services plus software.
Let's see, just simple.
I think we want to keep everything recognizable and not super ornamental.
We want it to be direct and straightforward.
However, it needs to be refined.
We feel that the current logo lacks some of that refinement.
It is bold.
It has some gravitas, but there's a certain finesse that it's currently missing.
Let's see.
Then bursting.
It's playful, delightful, free, exciting.
I think it's part of the culture as well.
We get a lot done.
The output is very vivid, very bright, very high quality, and we really want to make that shine.

However, whether that bursting is the growthx brand at face value or whether it's, for example, a layer deeper or how we feature our clients, that's something that I would love to think through with you all, of course.
And then it needs to also express, you know, some value, some premium look and feel overall because the service is a high value service.
And on tone, you know,
I have an expert, kind, direct, energizing, confident, raw, provoking.
It's like we want to come across as the experts in our field.
At the same time, we also want to do that by being a little bit contrarian maybe some of the time, having a little bit of tongue-in-cheek humor and not take ourselves too seriously.
And then from a personality perspective, let's say, we try to just describe the overall culture and majority of folks that we've hired so far.
the curiosity there is really, really important culture of being responsible.
And it's it's very, it's very autonomous.
try to keep everything, everything, everything small, brave, you know, it's like there's a lot of folks taking on big projects, a lot of risk, adaptable.
It's like we're an early stage startup, so we need to be, we need to be flexible and honest.
Now in general.
you.
In our communication and how we treat our customers and our fellow team members, and generous in mostly time towards our customers and our fellow team members there.
Then, yeah, thank you for screening here.
Inspiration is like a quick mood board that we put together.
We're in this silly AI era where the output of these models is getting stronger and stronger and stronger.
It's getting trained on a lot of materials that push a lot of this work to the verge of, you call it non-ethical altogether.
For us, we truly believe that human...

We need to be in the loop in order to do great work with this, and we want to make sure that the design that we establish looks back to, or takes some inspiration from other periods where pretty significant technological changes caused us to start to work differently, specifically.
And one of the things that we landed on was the transition specifically from hand-made, hand-manufactured graphic design to desktop publishing, digital desktop publishing.
It's the scale jump that designers made in the, you know, it's like moving from the 70s towards the 80s, towards the 90s, that caused them to work completely different.
It introduced the grid in a really, really deep and almost

Dogmatic way, you know, it's like Swiss, and it's like a lot of Western European graphic design, a lot of, you know, form follows function type of design, and essentially, you know, it's like template, the first things of template design to do a lot of it at scale very fast.
And we see similar things happening right now, but we want to bring in that same thoughtfulness when it comes to grid systems, systemic design, graphic design, strong typography.
We want that to resonate somehow in the brand that we're establishing.
So the current brand, I think, yeah, this is, so the, we,
I don't necessarily think that the logo is bad per se, you know, it's like I said before, it's like the weight is along the lines that we would like it.
But we feel that there could be perhaps some refinement.
There's a lot happening and there could be perhaps some more balance.
When it comes to the overall colorways typography, think the biggest gripe that we internally have around the current brand is that typographically, it's kind of all over the place.
We really want to see more uniformity there, perhaps less contrast between the sans serifs and the serifs that we have currently.
And perhaps a bit more unification of pulling whatever we use in the brand headline typography towards.
The logo mark, at least weight-wise, when you're just looking for a bit more uniformity in the overall brand.
But other than that, it's kind of, yeah, I wouldn't necessarily say loose, but I'm very interested in how you interpret this specifically, right?
It's like the mood board does not necessarily mean, like, hey, we need something that looks exactly like Joseph Miller-Brockman's, you know, grid systems book.

It's more about the philosophy and the, you know, the transition that folks had to make and the similar types of, you know, like grid systems and systemic design that will help a lot of this generative AI be successful within our context.
Let me pause there and see if that makes sense.
Well, me, Renato, it makes a lot of sense.
It looks like, well, not it looks like, it speaks for itself.
Like, you are a designer, right?
Yeah.
I mean, yeah.
So I think Camila and myself are very appreciative of how you are explaining your vision and all the resources that you, like, have created along the way.
So thank you.
Thank you for that.
you.
I do have a question.
Yes.
question is, how do you want people to feel when they see your brand?
Like, and I'm asking this because it was super interesting when you were mentioning about having this human approach and also this AI approach.
And you mentioned, like, the look and feel of the AI related companies nowadays, right?
Yeah.
So when you see a dominant brand.
Yeah.
A certain industry that look and feel also expands to many other brands and the new brands that are being created, right?
So my question is, how do you want people to feel when they see your brand in relation to the human approach and the AI approach?
Do you want a balance?
Do you want people to know that there is a human behind that brand?
Or would you like to make it more techy or, like, automatized, related, or something like that?
Yeah.
That's a really, really good question.
And I think that goes to the core of what we're trying to achieve.
And honestly, it's like I don't know whether I have the answer on that already.
I think what's important to us is that, I mean, a human in the loop is something that we believe in.
Needs to have humane properties, and by default, there needs to be details in there that cannot necessarily feel too machined, right?
And, on the other hand, when I want people that interact with us, I want them to feel that we are, we feel confident that we are experts at turning this AI into fantastic output.

We started playing around a little bit with ideas for the publication around this as well, some stuff, oh yeah, this is the output, we were thinking about, you know, what are specific visual or aesthetic metaphors, style treatments that do a good job at expressing AI
And how can we make that, yeah, convert or transition into something that feels more human or human-made, if you will.

And we were thinking about, like, perhaps that's, you know, like, perhaps part of it is pig-related or feels partially done, you know, like, or not entirely done just yet or half-generated or something.

It is definitely okay that there are imperfections to it and that there are things that make you think about the brand as a level deeper than perhaps, you know, it's like the Swiss design mood board here tells you.
But if we were to move into any direction, I would say, you know, it's like lean more into humanness than into, it's like generative, generative art.
But ideally, we'd see a little bit of a balance or interesting contrast.

Renato, one follow-up question there, like, what's the idea for the current brand using this black and white photography, was that the idea of showing a humane or analog approach next to this more techy vibe that you show by creating those UI elements?
Yeah, I think it was.
It wasn't present for the development of that, of those brand guidelines specifically, but that is a pretty, you know, it's like straightforward contrast.

It is pretty obvious, but it's a bit too obvious, know, it's like, to us, it's like, yeah, okay, there's retro elements as part of it, which is all right, but it may be too superficial, we'd like it to be.
be a level further integrated.
Thank you.
Also, Renato, I want to ask you specifically about the typography.
I noticed that you are using two very different styles.
For example, this one is the font.
I think it has like a very playful and friendly look and feel, would say.
But you are also talking about making the brand look more professional and an expert in what you do.
So I was wondering if this like playful touch is something that you want to keep in the overall identity, or do you want to look more professional and less playful?
The playfulness, definitely.
It used to be part of it, but it can be more of a detail.
Again, it's perhaps a level deeper or something that's not as prominent as it is right now.
Right now, on the growthx homepage, for example, there's a lot of use of sans-serif and sans-serif combinations.
That's kind of, I wouldn't even necessarily call it playful.
It just feels like a bit too much contrast.
I think overall, when it comes to typography, you're pretty open to new suggestions there as well.
I think the playfulness needs to be captured in terms of energy.
could also be captured through motion, for example.
It doesn't necessarily have to be a stand-up.
So, when it comes to typography, simplification is definitely what we'd prefer.

Ideally, moving to one type of font family for any marketing display types of usages, and we'll likely have our user interface typography, which will be, you it's like some type Or, you know, it's like Helvetica, Helvetica Friant.
Okay, thank you.
And how do you feel about this refund?
Like, it's that...
It's like, within this context of growthx, we feel it's a little, it's like in the space, it's a bit overdone.
So, we see a lot of templates that have, you know...
This combination of playful, bold font plus a more elegant serif, from that perspective, we prefer to move away from those trends a little bit.
Whether it's full serif or whether you propose serif versus sans serif, more open to that, more ambiguous to that from a core font family perspective.
But I think overall, it's like, we'll likely lean a little bit more towards the sense.
Okay, thank you.
I have one more question, if I may, Jannia, regarding the style and the content.
So, how important would be the communication of data for you, or for this plan?
And asking this, because that could help us.
Thank
So to better choose the font, a good font for that, I noticed that on the current website, you use, of course, lot of data or stats.

And so if that is going to remain something that you want to communicate, we can manage somehow to make that more prominent, more interesting, and perhaps that more infographic approach to the brand or something to me.
Yeah, I think for growth marketing specifically, metrics are absolutely, absolutely core to our customers.

That's what matters ultimately, the impact that the work has on their numbers, which is also one of the reasons why we knew that any designs that we would be making were requiring any systemic designs and branding would need to be, you know, it's like.

For information heavy purposes, so I definitely say yes on that, whether it's, yeah, and actually, like you say, there is an infographic they'll send over to, or perhaps it's part of the growthx brand guidelines right now, one of the pages in there.
I think, I'll send a separate link to it, but we have kind of like an infographic of how our system, how our system works.
I'll send that over and maybe a good one, a good one to use as well.
Great, thank you.
Yep.
All right, team.
Do you happen to have any questions from your end?
Not on.
We'll go here.
Yeah, go ahead.
No, no, nothing.
Sorry, Renato.
I heard you had anything to...
Yeah, I don't...
I do not think so.
I'm available to you all, just if you need anything to move forward.
It's like I'm a designer, so if you want to send me something quick just to get a gut reaction, feel free to just send it over.
I'm used to everything.
Other than that, I put my faith in your talented hands and hope to see something awesome come out of it.
No further questions.
Amazing.
Absolutely.
So next steps, we'll start to add you just to all of our ecosystems.
So...
We'll create a Slack channel so that we can have conversations and updates, just basically to troubleshoot.
We'll add you to Asana as well, that's where we handle all project management communications, you know, shares of updates and everything happens in Asana as well.
And yeah, we'll start sending over, we'll sync internally, we'll start fleshing out the project timeline so you can have at hand, you know, when is NBD going to send stuff.
And of course, you know, as we start fleshing everything out, if we have something that we want you to, you know, get your eyes on, like very quickly, is this the right direction?
so, we'll start sending those just to, you know, have a more open and collaborative space and see, you know, whether we're moving in the right direction or not.
But yeah, we'll start sending everything in the next couple hours.
Perfect, perfect, perfect.
Sorry, one question though.
So, apart from our our check-ins.
Do you anticipate any more synchronous time that needs to happen between us?
Any further sessions?
You mean for reviewing the work or just checking?
Yeah, outside of the check-in calls, you require any of my time?
Not at all unless you want us to go through the updates and present to you.
But other than that, there's no extra time yet.
Perfect.
All right.
Excellent.
Yeah.
Amazing.
Welcome.
And thanks a lot just for your time and input.
It'll make a difference.
I'm sure it will.
Yeah.
Thank you all for your time.
Thank you, Ren.
Nice to meet you, Renato.
Good to see you.
Bye.
Speak soon.
Bye-bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Kickoff meeting for GrowthX's rebranding project with Noboringdesign (NBD)](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=6.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX seeks a refined brand identity balancing human touch with AI expertise](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=563.0)
  - [Project timeline: \~1.5 months, covering kickoff, moodboarding, concepting, brand identity, and guidelines](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=412.0)
  - [Key brand pillars: bold, experimental, simple yet refined, bursting with energy, premium feel](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)
  - [Inspiration from 70s-90s transition to digital design; emphasis on grid systems and typography](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=1104.0)

## Topics

### GrowthX's Business Model and Brand Vision

  - [Delivers AI-first growth marketing services at scale](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=563.0)
  - [Building proprietary AI-enabled workflow software for marketing professionals](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=623.0)
  - [Seeks brand identity reflecting both human expertise and AI capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=698.0)
  - [Core values: bold, experimental, simple, refined, bursting with energy, premium](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=780.0)

### Current Brand Challenges

  - [Logo lacks refinement despite having appropriate weight](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=1189.0)
  - [Typography inconsistent and contrasting](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=1234.0)
  - [Desire for more uniformity in overall brand presentation](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=1215.0)
  - [Current black/white photography contrasts with tech elements, but feels superficial](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=1589.0)

### Design Direction and Inspiration

  - [Draw from 70s-90s transition to digital design era](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=1104.0)
  - [Incorporate grid systems, systemic design, strong typography](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=1132.0)
  - [Balance human touch with confident AI expertise](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=1451.0)
  - [Reduce playfulness in primary elements; consider motion for energy](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=1727.0)
  - [Simplify typography, potentially moving to one primary font family](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=1752.0)
  - [Data visualization and infographics important for showcasing metrics](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=1857.0)

### Project Timeline and Phases

1.  Kickoff (current meeting)
2.  Moodboarding (\~1 week)
3.  Concepting (\~2 weeks)
4.  Brand Identity Development
5.  Brand Guidelines (\~2 weeks)
6.  Final Deliverables: Figma file, PDF, necessary assets

## Next Steps

  - [NBD to add GrowthX team to Slack and Asana for project management](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=2040.0)
  - [NBD to flesh out detailed project timeline](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=2059.0)
  - [NBD to begin work on moodboarding phase](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=2087.0)
  - [Async communication for quick feedback on direction](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=2068.0)
  - [Regular check-in calls to be scheduled](https://fathom.video/share/vzEfX2MpFMsW__xCkj-Gfxs9yQjxhDRR?tab=summary&timestamp=2096.0)

action_items.description: Create Slack channel for GrowthX project, add Renato
Flesh out detailed project timeline for GrowthX rebrand
Send initial project setup info to Renato (Slack/Asana invites, timeline)
